strings.WINSLOW_MAP_FORLORNWRECKAGE_SUBTITLE: Lost Remnant of the UES 'Contact Light'
strings.WINSLOW_MAP_FORLORNWRECKAGE_BAZAAR_SEER: <style=cWorldEvent>You dream of an eternal cold, and shattered glass...</style>
strings.WINSLOW_MAP_FORLORNWRECKAGE_LORE:
<style=cMono>Welcome to the DataScraper (v3.1.54 – modded branch)
$ Scraping memory... done.
$ Resolving... done.
$ Combing for relevant data... done.
$ Scraping modded initials... done.
Outputting local audio transcriptions...
</style>

<style=cMono>Day 5</style>
<style=cMono>$ Begin Transmission</style>
We located the remaining parts of the Contact Light... James went *somewhere* he told me that he'd come back so I setup camp at one of the wrecks, I'd make a report to Safe Travels buuuut my transmitter got absolutely decimated by an encounter we had in a valley.. Oh! James is back okay can yo-
<style=cMono>$ Transmission Ended</style>

<style=cMono>Day 6</style>
<style=cMono>$ Begin Transmission</style>
Okay- so James and I got roughed up bad. We moved our camp behind one of the Cargo holds, we did sent a signal message to Safe Travels 4 days ago but we have not gotten a response since.. all we got ever since we sent it is garbled up noise with the occasional faint voices. The most clear thing we heard was something about a distress signal and... love? I don't know but while going through the cargo holds I found this old journal book thingy, it's very hard to read though due to the amount of time it spent here... I'll try to decypher what it says though, looks interesting.
<style=cMono>$ Transmission Ended</style>

<style=cMono>Day 8</style>
<style=cMono>$ Begin Transmission</style>
Alright so about the journal, I managed to decypher most of it and I believe it's about that massive structure in-front of the mountain in the distance? Let me read a part of it. “This bridge just goes on forever... but at least it is a bridge. Between this blizzard and the mountain fog I can't see more than two meters ahead, even with my mounted lights.“ Yeesh. That guy must have been unlucky... Anyway there are some nice photos of these creatures, some even I haven't seen before! Thank god! There's like a photo of this fire-y beast..? Looks like something straight out of mythology...
<style=cMono>$ Transmission Ended</style>

<style=cMono>Local text transcriptions complete.
</style>
